_note: Field names follow Jira Cloud REST API convention. Map 'epic_link' to your project's Epic Link custom field ID before import. 'story_points' maps to the Story Points custom field. Use Jira's bulk create CSV or REST API /rest/api/3/issue for actual import.

issues.summary: As a member, I want to register so that I can earn loyalty points
issues.description: Register a new member via POST /members. No input validation required. System auto-initializes point balances for all active partners.
acceptance_criteria: POST /members returns 201 with member ID and empty point balances
Point balance records initialized to 0 for KFC and McDonald's
MEMBER_REGISTERED event written to audit trail

issues.summary: As a member, I want to view my profile so that I can verify my registered information
issues.description: Retrieve a single member's detail via GET /members/{id}.
acceptance_criteria: GET /members/{id} returns 200 with full member details
Returns 404 when the member ID does not exist
Response includes memberId, name, email, status, and createdAt

issues.summary: As an admin, I want to list all members so that I can manage the member base
issues.description: Admin-only endpoint to retrieve a paginated list of all members via GET /members.
acceptance_criteria: GET /members returns 200 with a list of all members
Response supports pagination (page, size query params)
Requires admin authorization

issues.summary: As a developer, I want KFC and McDonald's partner data seeded so that the platform has baseline partners
issues.description: Use Flyway migration scripts to seed KFC and McDonald's records into the partners table on application startup.
acceptance_criteria: Flyway migration creates partner records for KFC and McDonald's
Migration is idempotent — running twice does not create duplicates
Partners are visible via GET /partners after startup

issues.summary: As a user, I want to list all partners so that I know which partners are available
issues.description: Public endpoint to retrieve all active loyalty partners via GET /partners.
acceptance_criteria: GET /partners returns 200 with a list of all partners
Each partner entry includes partnerId, name, and status
Only ACTIVE partners are returned

issues.summary: As a member, I want to submit a partner transaction so that I can earn loyalty points
issues.description: Submit a transaction to earn points via POST /transactions. Points are calculated from the transaction amount and the partner's accumulation rate.
acceptance_criteria: POST /transactions returns 201 with earned points and updated balance
Transaction record stored with memberId, partnerId, amount, pointsEarned, and timestamp
POINTS_EARNED event written to audit trail
Returns 400 if member status is INACTIVE

issues.summary: As the system, I want points calculated using the formula floor(trxAmount/1000) × rate so that earning is consistent
issues.description: Implement the points calculation formula: pointsEarned = floor(transactionAmount / 1000) × partnerRate. Rate is retrieved from the partner record.
acceptance_criteria: Points formula is floor(trxAmount / 1000) × partnerRate
Fractional points are always floored, never rounded
Changing the partner rate in DB immediately affects new transactions

issues.summary: As the system, I want point balances initialized to 0 on member registration so that new members are ready to earn
issues.description: When a new member registers, automatically create point balance records set to 0 for every active partner.
acceptance_criteria: Point balance records created for all active partners upon member registration
Initial balance is 0 for each partner
Adding a new partner does not retroactively create balances for existing members

issues.summary: As a member, I want to view my point balance per partner so that I know how many points I have
issues.description: Retrieve a member's point balance broken down by partner via GET /members/{id}/points.
acceptance_criteria: GET /members/{id}/points returns 200 with a list of {partnerId, partnerName, balance}
Returns 404 when member does not exist
Balance reflects all EARN, REDEEM, and EXCHANGE transactions

issues.summary: As the system, I want to reject transactions for INACTIVE members so that program integrity is maintained
issues.description: Before processing a point-earning transaction, verify the member's status. Reject the request with a descriptive error if the member is INACTIVE.
acceptance_criteria: POST /transactions returns 400 with error message when member status is INACTIVE
No transaction record is created for rejected requests
No audit event is emitted for rejected requests

issues.summary: As a member, I want to view the reward catalog so that I can decide what to redeem
issues.description: Retrieve all available rewards via GET /rewards. Rewards are pre-seeded via Flyway.
acceptance_criteria: GET /rewards returns 200 with a list of rewards
Each reward includes rewardId, name, partnerId, pointsCost, and stock
Only rewards with stock > 0 are returned by default

issues.summary: As a member, I want to redeem my points for a reward so that I get value from my loyalty
issues.description: Submit a redemption request via POST /redeem. System deducts the points cost from the member's balance and decrements reward stock.
acceptance_criteria: POST /redeem returns 201 with redemption ID and updated point balance
Member's point balance for the partner is deducted by the reward's pointsCost
Reward stock decremented by 1
POINTS_REDEEMED event written to audit trail
Returns 400 if balance is insufficient

issues.summary: As the system, I want to validate sufficient balance before redemption so that members cannot overdraft
issues.description: Before processing a redemption, verify that the member's point balance for the relevant partner is greater than or equal to the reward's pointsCost.
acceptance_criteria: Redemption proceeds only when balance >= reward pointsCost
Validation is atomic — no partial deduction occurs on failure
Returns a structured error body on validation failure

issues.summary: As a member, I want to receive a clear error on insufficient balance so that I understand why redemption failed
issues.description: Return a descriptive 400 error response when a redemption request cannot be fulfilled due to insufficient points.
acceptance_criteria: POST /redeem returns 400 with error code INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE
Response body includes current balance and required pointsCost
No balance change or stock change occurs on failure

issues.summary: As a member, I want to exchange points between partners so that I can consolidate my loyalty points
issues.description: Submit a point exchange request via POST /exchange. Points are deducted from the source partner and credited to the target partner after applying the exchange rate.
acceptance_criteria: POST /exchange returns 200 with both updated balances (source and target partner)
Source balance decremented by the exchanged amount
Target balance incremented by floor(amount × exchangeRate)
POINTS_EXCHANGED event written to audit trail
Returns 400 if source balance is insufficient

issues.summary: As the system, I want to look up exchange rates from the database so that rates can be updated without redeployment
issues.description: Exchange rates between partner pairs are stored in an exchange_rates table and retrieved at runtime during each exchange operation.
acceptance_criteria: Exchange rate is fetched from exchange_rates table by (fromPartnerId, toPartnerId)
Returns 400 with error EXCHANGE_RATE_NOT_FOUND if no rate is configured
Changing the rate in DB takes effect for the next exchange immediately

issues.summary: As a developer, I want default exchange rates seeded via Flyway so that the platform works out of the box
issues.description: Use a Flyway migration to seed default exchange rates: KFC→McDonald's at 0.8 and McDonald's→KFC at 1.25.
acceptance_criteria: Flyway migration inserts KFC→McDonald's rate of 0.8
Flyway migration inserts McDonald's→KFC rate of 1.25
Migration is idempotent

issues.summary: As a member, I want to see both updated balances after an exchange so that I can confirm the operation succeeded
issues.description: The POST /exchange response must include the updated point balances for both the source and target partner.
acceptance_criteria: Response includes sourcePartner balance after deduction
Response includes targetPartner balance after credit
Balances in response match values stored in DB

issues.summary: As a developer, I want 3-5 dummy rewards per partner seeded via Flyway so that the catalog is populated for testing
issues.description: Create a Flyway migration that inserts sample rewards for KFC and McDonald's into the rewards table.
acceptance_criteria: At least 3 rewards seeded per partner (KFC and McDonald's)
Each reward record includes name, pointsCost, stock, and partnerId
Migration is idempotent

issues.summary: As a member, I want to filter rewards by partner so that I only see relevant options
issues.description: Support a query parameter ?partnerId= on GET /rewards to filter the reward catalog to a specific partner.
acceptance_criteria: GET /rewards?partnerId={id} returns only rewards belonging to that partner
Returns empty list (not 404) when no rewards exist for the given partner
Returns 400 if partnerId is not a valid UUID

issues.summary: As a member, I want to view my paginated transaction history so that I can track my point activity
issues.description: Retrieve a member's full transaction history with pagination via GET /members/{id}/transactions.
acceptance_criteria: GET /members/{id}/transactions returns 200 with paginated list
Each entry includes transactionId, type, partnerId, amount, pointsChanged, and timestamp
Supports page and size query parameters
Results ordered by timestamp descending

issues.summary: As a member, I want to filter my transactions by type so that I can find specific activity
issues.description: Support a ?type= query parameter on GET /members/{id}/transactions to filter by transaction type (EARN, REDEEM, EXCHANGE_IN, EXCHANGE_OUT).
acceptance_criteria: GET /members/{id}/transactions?type=EARN returns only EARN transactions
Accepts EARN, REDEEM, EXCHANGE_IN, EXCHANGE_OUT as valid values
Returns 400 if an invalid type value is provided

issues.summary: As the system, I want to log a MEMBER_REGISTERED event on registration so that all registrations are traceable
issues.description: Emit a MEMBER_REGISTERED audit event whenever a new member is successfully registered.
acceptance_criteria: MEMBER_REGISTERED event written to audit_trail table after successful registration
Event record includes eventType, memberId, timestamp, and payload snapshot
Audit write failure must not roll back member creation (fire-and-forget or separate TX)

issues.summary: As the system, I want to log a POINTS_EARNED event on transaction so that all point earnings are auditable
issues.description: Emit a POINTS_EARNED audit event whenever points are successfully earned via a partner transaction.
acceptance_criteria: POINTS_EARNED event written to audit_trail after successful transaction
Event includes memberId, partnerId, transactionAmount, and pointsEarned
Event timestamp matches transaction timestamp

issues.summary: As the system, I want to log a POINTS_REDEEMED event on redemption so that all redemptions are auditable
issues.description: Emit a POINTS_REDEEMED audit event whenever points are successfully redeemed for a reward.
acceptance_criteria: POINTS_REDEEMED event written to audit_trail after successful redemption
Event includes memberId, rewardId, pointsDeducted, and partnerId
Event timestamp matches redemption timestamp

issues.summary: As the system, I want to log a POINTS_EXCHANGED event on exchange so that all exchanges are auditable
issues.description: Emit a POINTS_EXCHANGED audit event whenever points are successfully exchanged between partners.
acceptance_criteria: POINTS_EXCHANGED event written to audit_trail after successful exchange
Event includes memberId, fromPartnerId, toPartnerId, amountExchanged, and pointsCredited
Event timestamp matches exchange timestamp

issues.summary: As the system, I want to log MEMBER_UPDATED and MEMBER_STATUS_CHANGED events on CMS edits so that admin changes are auditable
issues.description: Emit appropriate audit events when an admin updates member details or toggles member status via CMS endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: MEMBER_UPDATED event emitted when PUT /members/{id} succeeds
MEMBER_STATUS_CHANGED event emitted when member status is toggled
Each event includes actorId (admin key), memberId, changedFields, and timestamp

issues.summary: As an admin, I want to edit member details so that I can correct member information
issues.description: Admin endpoint to update member details via PUT /members/{id}. Requires X-Admin-Key header for authorization.
acceptance_criteria: PUT /members/{id} returns 200 with updated member details
Requires valid X-Admin-Key header; returns 401 if missing or invalid
Returns 404 if member does not exist
MEMBER_UPDATED event written to audit trail

issues.summary: As an admin, I want to toggle member status between ACTIVE and INACTIVE so that I can manage member access
issues.description: Admin endpoint to change a member's status via PATCH /members/{id}/status. Requires X-Admin-Key header.
acceptance_criteria: PATCH /members/{id}/status accepts {status: ACTIVE|INACTIVE} in request body
Returns 200 with updated member status
Requires valid X-Admin-Key header; returns 401 if missing or invalid
MEMBER_STATUS_CHANGED event written to audit trail

issues.summary: As a developer, I want unit tests for PointService#earnPoints so that formula and exception paths are covered
issues.description: Write JUnit 5 unit tests for PointService#earnPoints covering the happy path formula calculation and all exception scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: Test covers correct points calculation using floor(trxAmount/1000) × rate
Test covers exception thrown when member is INACTIVE
Test covers exception thrown when member is not found
All tests pass with no external dependencies (Mockito mocks used)

issues.summary: As a developer, I want unit tests for PointService#redeemPoints so that success and insufficient balance paths are covered
issues.description: Write JUnit 5 unit tests for PointService#redeemPoints covering successful redemption and the insufficient balance error path.
acceptance_criteria: Test covers successful redemption with correct balance deduction
Test covers InsufficientBalanceException when balance < pointsCost
Test verifies stock is not decremented on failure
All tests pass with Mockito mocks

issues.summary: As a developer, I want unit tests for PointService#exchangePoints so that success and error paths are covered
issues.description: Write JUnit 5 unit tests for PointService#exchangePoints covering successful exchange and all error paths (insufficient balance, missing rate).
acceptance_criteria: Test covers successful exchange with correct deduction and credit
Test covers InsufficientBalanceException on low source balance
Test covers ExchangeRateNotFoundException when rate is missing
All tests pass with Mockito mocks

issues.summary: As a developer, I want unit tests for MemberService#registerMember so that registration logic is verified
issues.description: Write JUnit 5 unit tests for MemberService#registerMember covering member creation and point balance initialization.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies member entity is persisted via repository
Test verifies point balance records initialized for all active partners
Test verifies MEMBER_REGISTERED audit event is emitted
All tests pass with Mockito mocks

issues.summary: As a developer, I want unit tests for AuditTrailService#log so that audit logging is verified
issues.description: Write JUnit 5 unit tests for AuditTrailService#log to ensure audit events are persisted correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies audit event record is saved to the repository
Test verifies event payload includes all required fields (eventType, memberId, timestamp)
Test covers behavior when repository throws an exception
All tests pass with Mockito mocks
